text.title: ads-attribution-models Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ads-attribution-models as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Comparing platform-reported conversions directly across channels to determine which channel is "winning."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Platform totals are not deduplicated. A user clicking both Meta and Google appears in both reports as a unique conversion. The comparison is meaningless without a unified view.

failure-mode.description: Running an incrementality test for less than 2 weeks or adjusting budgets during the test.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Insufficient runtime produces statistically noisy results. Mid-test budget changes alter the treatment condition and make the holdout comparison invalid.

failure-mode.description: Trusting platform-reported totals for cross-channel budget decisions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Platform totals are over-counted by 20-40% due to cross-channel overlap. Decisions based on them systematically overspend on channels that appear more efficient than they are.

failure-mode.description: Using last-click as the sole attribution model for a multi-touch journey.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Last-click assigns zero credit to all upper-funnel touchpoints, leading to systematic under-investment in awareness and consideration channels.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Display and video channels drive brand recall that appears as direct traffic. Excluding view-through undervalues awareness spend.

failure-mode.description: Agent explains small variances (under 15%) with elaborate narratives before a minimum-variance threshold is met.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Small variances are noise from lookback window differences, timezone offsets, and sampling. Narratives on noise mislead decision-makers.

failure-mode.description: Agent presents platform-reported and GA4-modeled numbers side-by-side as comparable values without labeling the model and window for each.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: They use different lookback windows and deduplication logic. An unlabeled table implies comparability where none exists.

failure-mode.description: Agent computes a single "today" number using GA4 property timezone and Google Ads account timezone interchangeably.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: GA4 uses property timezone; Google Ads uses account timezone. These can differ by hours and produce off-by-one date errors in daily reports.

failure-mode.description: Agent reports data-driven attribution point estimates without confidence intervals or sample-size caveats.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Data-driven models have uncertainty that grows with low conversion volume. Point estimates without CIs create false precision and overconfident recommendations.
